Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated serum creatinine), and there is significant proteinuria and ketonuria. Urine is abnormal in appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite, leukocyte esterase). Low urine specific gravity suggests impaired concentrating ability. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with acute component; recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes.
